steps_en: Regarding what we have previously observed, we will omit instructions for the preparation of simple wines (of Wormwood, Gentian, Eucalyptus, etc.), which are nothing more than vinous tinctures. Instead, we will present some recipes for compound wines, as well as those for chinato wine, which is in very frequent use.
notes_en: This is an introductory section explaining the scope of the following recipes.

yield.note: approximate, after straining and addition of spirit

ingredients.name_it: Scorze di N. 5 limoni triturate con zucchero
ingredients.name_en: Zest of 5 lemons, ground with sugar

steps_en: After 24 hours of maceration, strain and squeeze the solids.
Add 200 ml of alcohol at 85° or cognac.

ingredients.name_it: Corteccia di china grigia finamente soppesata

steps_en: Pour the alcohol over the cinchona bark in a glass vessel.
After 48 hours of maceration, during which the liquid should be shaken frequently, add the wine.
After 8 days the wine is ready to drink.
Do not filter it, as leaving it in contact with the bark allows better extraction of its active principles.
Each time you use it, decant carefully the required quantity.
notes_en: This is a very tonic wine. Filtering is not recommended, as prolonged contact with the bark ensures better extraction of active compounds.

yield.note: approximate (1 part bark → 30 parts Marsala wine)

steps_en: Macerate the crushed cinchona bark in Marsala wine for 10 days.
notes_en: This is the method prescribed by the Official Pharmacopoeia (Farmacopea Ufficiale).
